This repo is still a bit bare (README needs to be added) but it has CI set up to run on buildkite (https://buildkite.com/julialang/linearalgebra-dot-jl/builds/18) and doc building on GHA. The external repo has all commits up to https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/commit/4709b6c48e79f6226e6dbee1b49bf7e563058ff7 included in it.

The reason for the move is to be able to focus issues and PRs and development regarding LinearAlgebra in one wanted","color":"105F6E","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2024-09-29T20:37:55Z","updated_at":"2024-10-27T15:49:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"On Julia 1.10.5

```julia
julia> T = (Vector{Tuple{Code}} where {Code<:Integer})
Array{Tuple{Code}, 1} where Code<:Integer

julia> eltype(T)
Any

julia> @inline ptreltype(::Type{Array{T,N}}) where {T,N} = T
ptreltype (generic function with 1 method)

julia> ptreltype(T)
ERROR: UndefVarError: `T` not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] ptreltype(::Type{Array{Tuple{Code}, 1} where Code<:Integer})
   @ Main ./REPL[6]:1
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[7]:1
```

Not entirely sure what I would expect here (actually I would expect a methoderror), but definitely not this.

cc @mofeing requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-12-29T14:47:18Z","updated_at":"2024-01-02T21:53:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"If using A and B together should automatically load C and it is also possible to make use of C on its own without both A and B, then it makes sense have A B and C as separate packages, and have an AB package extension depend on C. This avoids adding an unnecessary dep on C from A or B individually.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52663/reactions","total_count":3,"+1":3,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52663/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1874611455","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52663#issuecomment-1874611455","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52663","id":1874611455,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5vvEz_","user":{"login":"mofeing","id":15837247,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1ODM3MjQ3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/15837247?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing","html_url":"https://github.com/mofeing","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-02T21:53:11Z","updated_at":"2024-01-02T21:53:11Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Paraphrasing @ToucheSir,
> To elaborate, the case @mofeing and I are describing is one where package C actually operates as package B.C in practice. That is, it's a "plugin" spun off from the core package B for latency reasons. These plugin packages are not something end users should be importing, but they also don't make sense to have as a hard dep if their parent package is a weak dep of package A.
>
> So the ideas we're left with are:
> - Allow package extensions to have their own deps, such that we can include these plugin packages without adding extra compile time (thus defeating part of the reason for weak deps) or asking users to install and load a bunch of obscure (to them) packages to get three+ way extensions working
> - Have some sort of mechanism like Cargo's crate features (https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/features.html#dependency-features), such that parts of a monolithic package like CUDA or Makie can be turned on or off at compile time. This would remove the need for plugin packages to be their own separate things

2 examples:
- In my case, I have a package that provides a graph-like structure (but not a graph) and i want users to visualize it when they call `Makie.plot` but the package that implements graph visualization in `Makie` is `GraphMakie`. So either...
  1. I mark `GraphMakie` as a weakdep and force the user to know that they need to add and load `GraphMakie` (which in my opinion is an implementation-detail that the user should ignore).
  2. or i mark `GraphMakie` as a regular dep and `Makie` as the weakdep triggering the package extension (which abstracts away the details but forces the package extension to always precompile due to `GraphMakie` being present in the deps, which has `Makie` as a parent dep).
- Flux's case (https://github.com/FluxML/Flux.jl/pull/2268):
> `cuDNN` is required for certain `NNlib` functionality on GPU. But the `cuDNN` package is not meant to be loaded by end users, hence the complete lack of documentation. To my knowledge, it mostly exists as a separate package to make CUDA.jl lighter.
> But because extensions can't have deps, we're forced to make an extension with just CUDA.jl and one with CUDA.jl + cuDNN. Asking users to install and import a random package every time instead of just using Flux, CUDA is bad UX, so we do a trick where the CUDA extension tries to load cuDNN and warns the user if it's not installed. But this is imperfect at best, because I believe it requires cuDNN to be installed in the current project environment

@ericphanson suggested that...
> I think that still aligns with my point of view that maybe the missing feature is for registered packages to also be allowed to act as extensions and get autoloaded when certain other packages are loaded

@vchuravy pointed out that would be isomorphic to dependencies for package extensions, and the latest is preferable to avoid dependency cycles because package extensions have a implicit dependency on their parent.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1874611455/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

++++
+mintoclevel = 2
+maxtoclevel = 3
+title = "Julia 1.10 Highlights"
+authors = "The Julia contributors"
+published = "22 Dec 2023"
+rss_pubdate = Date(2023, 12, 22)
+rss = """Highlights of the Julia 1.10 release."""
++++
+
+
+After X betas and X release candidates, Julia version 1.10 has finally(!) been released. We would like to thank all the contributors to this release and all the testers that helped with finding regressions and issues in the pre-releases. Without you, this release would not have been possible.
+
+The full list of changes can be found in the [NEWS file](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/release-1.9/NEWS.md), but here we'll give a more in-depth overview of some of the release highlights.
+
+\toc
+
+# New parser written in Julia
+
+*Claire Foster*
+
+In Julia 1.10 we have replaced Julia's default parser (written in Scheme) with one written in Julia...
+
+# Package load time improvements
+
+*Jameson Nash et al.*
+
+In 1.9 the TTFX was heavily improved by allowing package authors to enable saving of native code during precompilation.
+
+In 1.10, as a follow up, signficant work was put into the performance of the loading of packages
+
+A lot of this work was driven by profiling and improving the load time of [OmniPackage.jl](https://github.com/JuliaComputing/OmniPackage.jl) which is an artifical "mega package" which only purpose is to depend on and load a lot of dependencies.
+
+- Improvement to the type system... (Jameson)
+- Reudction of invalidations causing recompilation
+- Moving packages away from Requires.jl to package extensions
+- `mul!` dispatch improvements (Daniel K)
+
+
+# Stacktrace rendering improvements
+
+*Jeff Bezanson, Tim Holy*
+
+- `Type{...}` in stacktraces
+- Successive frames at identical location collapsed
+- https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49102
+
+
+# Parallel GC
+
+*Diogo Netto*
+
+Up to 1.9, Julia's garbage collector was stop-the-world (meaning that all compute threads had to be stopped while a collection cycle was running) and serial (meaning a single thread performed a collection while the compute threads were halted). As one may imagine, garbage collection can quickly become a bottleneck in multithreaded code, particularly in code where multiple threads allocate.
+
+In 1.10, we gave the first steps towards improving scalability the of garbage collector in multithreaded code. More specifically, our work consisted of parallelizing the mark-phase of Julia's GC and enabling part of sweeping to be run concurrently with compute threads.","path":"blog/2023/12/julia-1.10-highlights.md","commit_id":"85bc151e3ab53a896e473523116cc24de47526cd","original_commit_id":"85bc151e3ab53a896e473523116cc24de47526cd","user":{"login":"mofeing","id":15837247,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1ODM3MjQ3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/15837247?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing","html_url":"https://github.com/mofeing","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mofeing/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/28567","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/28567/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/28567/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/28567/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/28567","id":349604180,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWUzNDk2MDQxODA=","number":28567,"title":"Performance of immutable vs mutable unions","user":{"login":"bkamins","id":6187170,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxODcxNzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6187170?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins","html_url":"https://github.com/bkamins","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bkamins/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":115858,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTU4NTg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/performance","name":"performance","color":"d7e102","default":false,"description":"Must go faster"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2018-08-10T17:52:57Z","updated_at":"2023-10-05T17:35:57Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"In some situations using an array of union of immutable types is slower than using and array of union of mutable types.

In the code below three functions `f`, `g` and `h` do the same operation on array of union of immutables or mutables. Functions `f` and `h` are much slower in union of immutables case. Function `g` behaves as expected.

Here is the example:
```
struct A1
    x::Int
end

struct A2
    x::Int32
end

mutable struct B1
    x::Int
end

mutable struct B2
    x::Int32
end

a = Union{A1,A2}[isodd(i) ? A1(i) : A2(i) for i in 1:10^6]
b = Union{B1,B2}[isodd(i) ? B1(i) : B2(i) for i in 1:10^6]

function f(z)
    s = 0
    for v in z
        s += v.x
    end
    s
end

g(a) = sum(v -> v.x, a)
h(a) = sum(v.x for v in a)
```

And here are benchmark results on Julia 1.0:
```
julia> using BenchmarkTools

julia> @btime f($a)
  4.214 ms (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
500000500000

julia> @btime f($b)
  1.728 ms (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
500000500000

julia> @btime g($a)
  1.578 ms (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
500000500000

julia> @btime g($b)
  1.757 ms (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
500000500000

julia> @btime h($a)
  4.783 ms (1 allocation: 16 bytes)
500000500000

julia> @btime h($b)
  1.782 ms (1 allocation: 16 bytes)
500000500000
```

What I want is that the user doesn't need to do `using GraphMakie`, but that by just `using Makie` the extension that uses `GraphMakie` is loaded. I'm looking for this because I'm specializing the `Makie.plot!` method for my types.

The only available solution currently is to add `GraphMakie` as strong dependency, add `Makie` as weak dependency but only use `Makie` as the trigger for the extension (which I'm doing now)

This solution has the inconvenience that because `GraphMakie` is a strong dependency (and thus, `Makie`), the extension is always precompiled.
